source_summary: Internal GC meeting notes on Q4 workforce allocation—how to staff hospital, school, and multifamily work with limited senior superintendents, slow HR onboarding, safety/training gaps, and schedule/client risk.
decision_context: Portfolio-level workforce allocation under Q4 schedule pressure: a hospital project needs an experienced senior superintendent, a school project faces penalties without strong field leadership, multifamily closeout is holding Marcus longer than planned, HR cannot fill gaps immediately, and promotion/travel/subcontract options each carry safety, cost, and retention tradeoffs.
stated_decision: Decide how to allocate field leadership and hiring, promotion, subcontracting, and de-prioritization actions for Q4 work before the monthly staffing review.
implied_decision: Whether to protect the highest-risk hospital project by reallocating Marcus even if lower-priority work and the school project receive weaker superintendent coverage.
goals: Staff highest-risk projects with qualified field leadership
Avoid schedule penalties on the school and hospital work
Protect safety and quality across active jobs
Avoid burnout and attrition among senior field leaders
Make promotion and hiring decisions explicit before client-facing commitments
options_considered: Reassign senior superintendent Marcus to the hospital project once multifamily closeout can transition
Promote foreman Carlos to assistant superintendent on the school project with a defined training plan
Hire a traveling superintendent or contract superintendent for the hospital start
Subcontract self-perform concrete scope temporarily on the lower-priority warehouse job
Delay or de-prioritize lower-risk warehouse work
Split superintendent coverage across hospital and school critical paths temporarily
constraints: Hospital project start is approaching with a client call requiring a named superintendent
School schedule has minimal float on key milestones
HR candidates cannot start immediately and onboarding is slow
Self-perform concrete crew is already overcommitted
Decision is needed before the monthly staffing review and Thursday client call
risks: Overloading senior superintendents such as Marcus
Safety and compliance gaps if promotion happens before training is complete
Client escalation on hospital or school projects
Schedule penalties if experienced field leadership is pulled
Attrition from travel fatigue and repeated reassignments
Weak supervision on lower-priority projects during reallocation
assumptions: HR candidates cannot start immediately enough for the hospital date
The hospital project carries the highest delivery and client risk
Promotion candidate Carlos can succeed with support and training
Core project dates are not fully movable
evidence: Hospital client is asking for a named senior superintendent with healthcare experience
School project controls reports minimal float on structural milestones
Multifamily closeout is slipping and keeping Marcus longer than planned
HR has two superintendent candidates but neither can start next week
Safety/training requirements for Carlos promotion are not yet resolved
Self-perform concrete crew is overcommitted across active work
open_questions: What is the confirmed hospital project start date from the owner?
When can HR candidates realistically start and at what cost?
What is Carlos's certification and training status for school and potential hospital support?
What is the real schedule float on the school project?
Will clients tolerate a change in named superintendent coverage?
Can multifamily closeout transition to PM-led coverage without quality risk?
tensions: Hospital client urgency versus school penalty exposure if leadership is moved
Promoting Carlos quickly versus safety/training readiness
Traveling superintendent cost versus retention and local candidate gaps
Subcontracting concrete to free capacity versus margin and operational control
Protecting senior leaders from burnout versus covering every active job strongly
recommendation_candidate: Assign Marcus to the hospital project with an explicit multifamily handoff plan. Promote Carlos to assistant superintendent on the school project only with Safety/Training sign-off. Activate an HR backstop hire for superintendent coverage. Delay warehouse work or subcontract concrete scope if float cannot be protected.
missing_context: Confirmed hospital and school start dates and owner commitments
Candidate availability and hiring timeline from HR
Certification and training status for promotion and hospital requirements
Real schedule float on each affected project
Client tolerance for superintendent coverage changes
suggested_next_steps: Finalize a Q4 staffing matrix before the monthly staffing review
Confirm schedule float with project controls on school and hospital work
Decide promotion and foreman backfill plan for Carlos with named owners
Validate safety and training readiness before any promotion or reassignment
Assign HR owner for traveling or contract superintendent hiring path
Revisit allocation decisions at the next workforce planning meeting
